title: From Xenium RNA to CosMx Protein
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"nvgg","text":"This protocol details the transition of Xenium RNA to CosMx protein.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"4jj77","text":"Visium CytAssist Spatial Gene Expression (CG000709 Rev A, 10X Genomics)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"datpk","text":"CosMx SMI Manual Slide Preparation for Protein Assays (MAN-10185-01-1, NanoString)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"crbc7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Following completion of the Xenium RNA run, slides were removed from the instrument and stored in the Xenium cassette with PBS-T at 4 °C .

After storage for up to 3 days, slides were washed three times in PBS-T for 1 minute each as recommended in Technical Note: Post-Xenium In Situ Applications: Immunofluorescence, H&E, and Visium CytAssist Spatial Gene Expression (CG000709 Rev A, 10X Genomics), removed from the Xenium cassette and placed into a wash jar of 1X PBS for 00:05:00 as described in CosMx SMI Manual Slide Preparation for Protein Assays (MAN-10185-01-1, NanoString), incubation frames were attached to the Xenium slide and tissue sections were covered with Buffer W and incubated at Room temperature for 01:00:00 in the dark.
The slides were washed three times in PBS-T for 00:01:00 (1/3).
The slides were washed three times in PBS-T for 00:01:00 (2/3).
The slides were washed three times in PBS-T for 00:01:00 (3/3).
Afterwards, MAN-10185-01-1 and MAN-10161-03-2 were followed as previously described in CosMx Protein slide preparation and CosMx SMI setup and data acquisition.
creator: Spatial Touchstone
